course.title: Learn Python
course.description: Master Python from scratch with hands-on interactive coding lessons. From variables to machine learning.

chapter.title: 7. Error Handling & Files
chapter.description: Learn to gracefully handle errors and read/write to files.

lessons.title: Try Except Blocks
lessons.explanation: When an error occurs, Python normally stops and generates an error message (an Exception). You can handle these exceptions using a `try...except` block, allowing your program to continue running or fail gracefully.
lessons.analogy: A try block is like a safety net for a trapeze artist. If the trick (the code) fails and they fall (an error occurs), the safety net (except block) catches them so the show can go on.
common_mistakes: Using a bare `except:` without specifying the error type, which can hide unexpected bugs.
best_practices: Always try to catch specific exceptions (like ValueError or ZeroDivisionError) rather than all exceptions.
lessons.summary: Try and Except blocks are critical for building robust applications that don't crash when users provide bad input.

examples.title: Handling Division by Zero
examples.explanation: Normally, 10/0 would crash the program. The try/except catches the ZeroDivisionError, prints a friendly message, and lets the program continue.

exercises.statement: Write a try/except block that tries to print an undefined variable named `unknown_var`. Catch the `NameError` and print 'Variable not found'.
